item.title: Reported attack hits South Pars natural gas field, an energy lifeline for Iran
item.description: Iranian state media said Wednesday that Israel attacked Iran's South Pars natural gas field — part of the world's largest such gas resource and a pillar of the country's energy supplies. Facilities associated with the gas field near Asaluyeh on Iran's Gulf coastline were on fire on Wednesday, state media reported, and Iran vowed it would attack energy facilities in other Gulf countries in retaliation.

item.title: Iraqi Kurdish shepherds stoic in face of yet another war
item.description: Through one conflict after another, herders in Iraqi Kurdistan have grazed their animals under the endless blue skies of the Zagros Mountains -- a lifestyle they are stoically trying to maintain as yet another war engulfs the region.But we have no choice: we have to graze the animals," he said.

item.title: US launches new era of drug war with Latin American allies
item.description: The United States and allies in Latin America and the Caribbean have ushered in a new phase of anti-drug cooperation including extraditions, shared intelligence and security plans despite criticism from international organizations.The anti-drug campaign, which includes controversial elements such as attacks on suspected drug-smuggling boats in the Caribbean, is sparking alarm in some parts of the region.

item.title: Belgium deploys military to guard Jewish sites after Iran-linked group claims Europe attacks
item.description: Belgium deploys military to protect Jewish communities after explosion outside Liège, Belgium, synagogue. Officials call blast a "despicable antisemitic" act.

item.title: Belgian soldiers to protect Jewish sites as Iran war fuels antisemitism
item.description: Source: CBS News

item.title: Booms, threat warning in Saudi capital Riyadh ahead of foreign ministers' meeting
item.description: By Timour Azhari March 18 (Reuters) - Saudi air defences dealt with a "ballistic threat" in Riyadh on Wednesday, state TV said, after several loud booms were heard and some of the city's residents

item.title: Iran’s new supreme leader issues a rare statement, sends condolences for slain top security official Larijani
item.description: Iran's new supreme leader issues a rare statement, sends condolences for slain top security official Larijani.

item.title: How many cargo ships are passing Hormuz strait?
item.description: Just a trickle of cargo ships and tankers has made it through the Strait of Hormuz since Iranian forces blocked the crucial trade route in the Middle East war.From March 1 to 18, commodities carriers made just 105 crossings, according to analytics firm Kpler -- a decrease of more than 95 percent. - 60 oil, gas tankers - Sixty of them were oil and gas tankers and of those nearly six out of 10 were loaded, Kpler data showed.

item.title: Foreign press group slams Israeli police for breaking journalist's wrist
item.description: An international media association on Wednesday criticised an "unprovoked assault" by Israeli police on journalists in Jerusalem, which it said left a CNN producer with a fractured wrist."During the assault, one Israeli officer fractured the wrist of a CNN producer," the FPA said in a statement.

item.title: US assesses China not planning to invade Taiwan in 2027
item.description: By Michael Martina WASHINGTON, March 18 (Reuters) - China does not currently plan to invade Taiwan in 2027 and seeks to control the island without the use of force, the U.S. intelligence community

item.title: Pakistan and Afghanistan agree to temporary Eid al-Fitr ‘pause’ in conflict
item.description: Pakistani minister says five-day pause, to begin at midnight Thursday, requested by Saudi Arabia, Qatar and Turkiye.

item.title: Kabul rehab centre patients fear for their future after attack that killed scores
item.description: By Mohammad Yunus Yawar and Ariba Shahid KABUL/KARACHI, March 18 (Reuters) - Former heroin user Nazar Mohammad said the Kabul rehabilitation centre bombed by Pakistan cured him of his drug addiction

item.title: UNICEF says it's investigating Israel’s allegations of smuggled tobacco in a Gaza aid shipment
item.description: UNICEF on Wednesday said it was launching an investigation into Israel’s allegation that tobacco products were smuggled into Gaza in one of the U.N. agency’s aid shipments heading to the territory. COGAT, the Israeli military body in charge of facilitating aid to Gaza, said Monday it found bottles containing tobacco substances hidden inside cartons of hygiene kits belonging to UNICEF. It said it had suspended the delivery of UNICEF aid to Gaza.

item.title: Iran was not rebuilding nuclear enrichment, US intelligence finds
item.description: US intelligence concluded Wednesday that Iran was not rebuilding nuclear enrichment capacities destroyed last year by the United States and Israel, contradicting a key justification by President Donald Trump for his ongoing war.Trump said after the June 2025 bombing that the United States had completely destroyed Iran's nuclear sites, but since his latest war he has maintained that Tehran was nonetheless weeks away from a nuclear bomb and that he had to act.

item.title: Countries propose safe corridor to free 20,000 seafarers stranded in Gulf
item.description: Source: Reuters

item.title: Colombia, Ecuador examining possible border violation during security operation amid presidential spat
item.description: By Luis Jaime Acosta BOGOTA, March 18 (Reuters) - Explosions in cocaine labs near the border with Ecuador killed 14 people in January, Colombia's Defense Minister Pedro Sanchez said on Wednesday, when

item.title: At least 80 insurgents killed as Nigerian troops repel base assault, military says

item.title: Guards consolidating power in Mojtaba Khamenei's Iran
item.description: The upheaval in Iran's leadership since the start of the war with Israel and the United States has further strengthened the political influence of the Islamic republic's ideological army, the Revolutionary Guards, analysts said.Despite the tumultuous changes in Iran's leadership over the last fortnight, the Guards have remained in a state of constant activity, with their various branches releasing statements boasting of new strikes against Israel, enforcing a de-facto blockade of the Strait of H

item.title: Afghanistan announces a temporary pause in fighting against Pakistan ahead of the Muslim holiday of Eid al-Fitr
item.description: Afghanistan announces a temporary pause in fighting against Pakistan ahead of the Muslim holiday of Eid al-Fitr.

item.title: Maiduguri bombings follow surge of jihadist violence in Nigeria
item.description: Nigeria's defence chiefs visited Maiduguri Wednesday after one of the deadliest attacks in the Borno state capital in years, in a show of defiance.In Maiduguri, chief of defence staff General Olufemi Oluyede pledged "in the future this will not repeat itself."

item.title: One person dead after gondola crashes down mountain at Swiss ski resort
item.description: ENGELBERG, Switzerland, March 18 (Reuters) - One person has died after a cable car cabin at a Swiss ski resort crashed down a snowy mountainside during strong winds on Wednesday.

item.description: WASHINGTON, March 18 (Reuters) - Democratic Senator Mark Warner assailed the U.S. intelligence community on Wednesday for not providing briefings or assessment on foreign threats to upcoming U.S.

item.title: Pakistan announces a 'temporary pause' in strikes against Afghanistan at the request of several countries
item.description: Pakistan announces a 'temporary pause' in strikes against Afghanistan at the request of several countries.

item.title: Troubled waters: Thai fishermen marooned by rising fuel costs
item.description: With his belongings stuffed into a plastic bag, Thai fisherman Narongsak Kongsuk heads home, far from the sea.His boss, Kwanchai Phatisena, has reluctantly decided to leave his boat moored for at least two weeks at the Sriracha jetty, north of the Thai resort city of Pattaya, where egrets and stray cats compete for fish that have fallen from plastic tubs.

item.title: Iranian state television confirms Israel killed Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib
item.description: Iranian state television confirms Israel killed Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib.

item.title: Amid ruins, Palestinians struggle to preserve Gaza’s historic markets
item.description: For centuries, the Grain Market has been Khan Younis’s shopping hub, but it has stood largely empty since Israel’s war.

item.description: By Jarrett Renshaw WASHINGTON, March 18 (Reuters) - The Trump administration on Wednesday announced a 60-day waiver of Jones Act shipping regulations to help ease deliveries of fuel and fertilizer to

item.title: Spain's Sanchez tells Ukraine's Zelenskiy that Iran war will not derail support
item.description: MADRID, March 18 (Reuters) - Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez said on Wednesday that the crisis in the Middle East would not distract from his country's support for Ukraine in its battle against

item.title: South Lebanon residents flee death and destruction
item.description: The line of cars stretched as far as the eye could see along the coast of southern Lebanon, as residents of areas bombarded in the Israel-Hezbollah war poured into the ancient city of Sidon in search of safety.For now the military says it is only conducting "limited" ground operations against Hezbollah in southern Lebanon.

item.title: Fiery aftermath of Iran missile strike near Tel Aviv caught on video after 2 killed
item.description: Video footage captured fiery destruction and emergency crews responding after an Iranian missile strike near Tel Aviv, Israel, killed two people.

item.title: German foreign ministry strongly discourages travel to Cuba
item.description: Source: Reuters

item.title: Russia condemns U.S.-Israeli strike near Iran's Bushehr nuclear plant reactor
item.description: MOSCOW, March 18 (Reuters) - Russia on Wednesday condemned a strike near Iran's Bushehr nuclear power plant a day earlier which it said was just metres away from its reactor and called on the United

item.title: Nigerian president meets royals on 'historic' UK state visit
item.description: King Charles III ceremonially welcomed Nigerian President Bola Tinubu at Windsor Castle on Wednesday in the first state visit by the leader of Africa's most populous nation in nearly four decades.The last Nigerian state visit to the UK took place in 1989, although Tinubu was received by Charles in September 2024.

item.title: Many in Egypt struggle as the costs of a distant war drive up prices in local markets
item.description: Sayyed Ragheb was already struggling to keep his family afloat, earning less than $100 a month. Now he fears it will get even worse after Egypt hiked fuel prices because of the Iran war. Egypt is one of the few countries in the Middle East not directly affected by the war, now in its third week with no sign of abating.

item.title: Japan's leader heads to Washington for a visit complicated by the Iran war fallout
item.description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is traveling Wednesday to the United States for what she expects to be a “very difficult” meeting with U.S. President Donald Trump after he called on Japan and other allies to send warships to secure the Strait of Hormuz. The three-day visit to Washington was originally expected to focus on trade and strengthening the U.S.-Japanese alliance as China’s influence grows in Asia. ”I think the U.S. visit will be a very difficult one, but I will do everything to maximize our national interest and to protect the daily lives of the people when the situation changes daily," Takaichi told parliament on Wednesday, hours before her departure.

item.title: What to know about the meningitis outbreak in England causing angst among university students
item.description: A meningitis outbreak in southeast England has led to the deaths of a university student and a pupil from a nearby school, prompting public health officials to quickly roll out medical interventions. The outbreak in the county of Kent was described Wednesday as unprecedented by U.K. Health Secretary Wes Streeting, owing to the high number of cases appearing in such a short space of time. The number of meningitis cases rose by a further five on Tuesday to 20.

item.title: Woman hospitalized after being startled by humanoid robot
item.description: Video widely shared online showed a woman angrily confronting a robot as it waved its metallic arms at her, while a crowd of onlookers gathered around.
